audit_items.proof_trace: q6asm_audio_client_release (the kref callback invoked by q6asm_audio_client_free) only calls kfree(ac) and never frees ac->port[0].buf or ac->port[1].buf. These audio_buffer arrays are allocated by q6asm_map_memory_regions (via kzalloc at L516) and are normally freed by q6asm_unmap_memory_regions → q6asm_audio_client_free_buf, but q6asm_unmap_memory_regions skips q6asm_audio_client_free_buf when __q6asm_memory_unmap fails (L407-411 goto err path). Callers (q6asm_dai_close L454-456, q6asm_dai_compr_free L683-685) ignore the unmap return value and call q6asm_audio_client_free unconditionally, leaving port->buf leaked.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker with access to the PCM audio device triggers q6asm_map_memory_regions (allocating port->buf), then forces an APR send failure during q6asm_unmap_memory_regions (e.g., via DSP error or device stress), causing q6asm_audio_client_free_buf to be skipped; q6asm_audio_client_free then runs and kfree(ac) leaks the port buffer. Repeated prepare/close cycles under this error condition exhaust kernel memory.
